title: Fluffy Nutty Egyptian Almond Pilaf
description: A fluffy, nutty rice pilaf packed with protein and aromatic spices, perfect for a post‑workout meal and sugar‑free friendly.
instructions: Rinse the rice under cold water until the water runs clear, then soak it in a bowl for 30 minutes.
While the rice soaks, combine the almonds, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, ground cloves, and lemon juice in a bowl; let them marinate for 15 minutes.
Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat and sauté the diced onion until translucent.
Add the minced garlic, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and the marinated almonds to the saucepan; cook, stirring, for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Drain the soaked rice and add it to the saucepan, stirring to coat the grains with the almond‑spice mixture.
Pour in the vegetable broth, bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Remove the pan from the heat, fluff the pilaf with a fork, then stir in the toasted almonds.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh parsley or a dollop of Greek yogurt if desired.
